item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This site just never runs out of delightfully weird birds, does it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool, funny, and like the 2nd bird scp selected in a row. Good job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't have to read any of those to fully understand and enjoy this article. I also don't know what you mean by "excessive" amounts of crosslinks as are there are only 4. Crosslinks are there to help you find new SCPs to read and to create a sense of continuity and story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the constant crosslinking is excessive and i'm not even past the first description paragraph and already have 4 other pages being thrust in my face to read ahead of time. it's distracting and it makes it difficult for me to concentrate on this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite like this. While I tend to prefer horror based SCP's and dislike more comedic ones, I like what this implies. I say this is how you do a sillier SCP; implement an intriguing idea, in this case an alternate dimension of bird people.

If I had to find fault with it it's that I think the conversation could've been more subtle and realistic. The person on the other line calls other people "it" and always specifies what type of bird they are. This doesn't make sense and, in my opinion, makes the idea a bit too obvious. I don't think the author was going for a subtle delivery, though, but I think the story would benefit from such.

Overall, it's a quite compelling idea with an interesting take on an otherwise straight forward premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha, how silly this is. Creative though.
And two bird related featured SCPs in a row, how about that

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
General note… yes, this page was technically featured once before, for about a day or so in 2016, for "Draw a picture of a bird day" way back when event features were done differently.

Now it gets a month feature because I didn't double-check my records. Mea culpa.

Happy October, everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My enthusiastic upvote became less enthusiastic as the article progressed, culminating with a novote after the addenda. The absurdity of the concept reminds me of 3900, in a good way, but it just isn't as good at using its complexity to its advantage. This would be fine, but the addenda drag the concept down with body-horror/backstory that feel both convoluted and shoehorned in.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a couple potential crosslinks we can work into here. The first, is turning the mention of Site-77 into a link to the site dossier. Second, it is mentioned up thread that "Spix's Macaw" may be a reference to SCP-2076, so we can inline link there. Finally, in an earlier wikiwalk crosslink, there was mention of using the birdphones to track the location of origin for phone call based anomalies, like SCP-1750, so we can make a similar mention here about such use of the object. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Update: As per Incident-2232-01, SCP-2232-03 has been neutralised. Samples of its remains are currently stored in the Inert Biological Sample wing of Site-77.

To this:

Update: As per Incident-2232-01, SCP-2232-03 has been neutralised. Samples of its remains are currently stored in the Inert Biological Sample wing of Site-77.

Changing this:

SCP-2232-08: Yeah, it just swerved into my lane, and didn't even sound the horn or anything, or signal, and the headlights and the passenger door on the old cassowary was just all busted up for weeks, I had to pay the repairmen, the insurance flamingo was simply insane, and - sorry, it's been a rough few months for me, listen. I've got some very important contacts and pictures on that bird, it means a lot to me, believe me. If there's a nearby Australian bush turkey you can drop it in a package and just put it in the interstate mail slot to number 23 [unintelligible - presumed to be an address], send it right to the doorstep of my Spix's macaw. That's okay with you, right?

To this:

SCP-2232-08: Yeah, it just swerved into my lane, and didn't even sound the horn or anything, or signal, and the headlights and the passenger door on the old cassowary was just all busted up for weeks, I had to pay the repairmen, the insurance flamingo was simply insane, and - sorry, it's been a rough few months for me, listen. I've got some very important contacts and pictures on that bird, it means a lot to me, believe me. If there's a nearby Australian bush turkey you can drop it in a package and just put it in the interstate mail slot to number 23 [unintelligible - presumed to be an address], send it right to the doorstep of my Spix's macaw. That's okay with you, right?

Adding the following to the Containment Procedures:

Use of SCP-2232 instances for tracking telephone based anomalies, including SCP-1750, has been approved on a case by case basis.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But then WHO/WHAT CALLED THE PHONE?!? Is there a secret colony of SCP-1000 the foundation doesn't know about? Definitely going in my head canon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would turning the phone upside down be rude?

geddit cause you're flipping the bird

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a soft spot for bird SCPs, especially talking ones (cack), and this one is funny and cute while still mainting the right level of strangeness.

That said, I can't decide if I'm happy or disappointed that Airplane Mode didn't do the obvious, and instead resulted in a Pelican.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: found this at an art show a week or two back. Happy +100, birdphone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really fun, and the body horror was…well…horriffic.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha what the heck is this, +1 for being weird but in a good way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good drawing. The "is the word" really pulls it together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, you should write a tale about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From minmin's author page, it is fully intentional. WingTel = SingTel.

I'm calling it: The reality that makes SCP-2232 is an alternate reality Singapore!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think this was intentional, but the "Wingtel" company stated in the interview log sounds like a play on words on the name "Singtel."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait so its Draw a picture of a bird day huh..the Irony compels me.
to do this

also I just love those whimsical type SCPs that always reminds me even though the world is scary in-universe but there is this type of wonder that always inspires. +1 anyday all day

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WUT

This is so wonderfully bizarre, also reminds me of a dream I had where I was holding a bird and like manipulating it, I was trying to find a window to let it out of and I was afraid if I wasnt careful I would hurt it or something.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Birds are everything. BIRDS ARE ALL THINGS. THAT MUST BE HELL.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… so this was another one of SCP-1000's technology…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They made things that we still can't comprehend, even though we've thoroughly studied the surviving pieces. Organic technology. They made trees and birds of prey grow into fast-moving ships, herds of animals that became trains, bushes that became flying vehicles. From insects and pigeons they made things equivalent to cell phones, televisions, computers.

From SCP-1000 - thanks, Dolphinslugchugger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what did i just read

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this because it is hilarious and makes perfect internal sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm tickled to think that Birdphone is implicated in planet-scale retroactive genocide and the essential survival of the human race.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I demand crosstests with Dr. Spanko. Thank you.

+1 for all the wat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mention of Spix's Macaw is inspiring. I'm tempted to turn SCP-2289-6 into a Spix's Macaw just because.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in chat a while back. I loved it. The changes really improved the article, in my opinion.

A hearty upvote, +01100010011010010111001101100100

(+bird)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This, ladies, gentlemen and otherwise, is the kind of wacky shit you need to come up with to stand out in today's Foundation wiki.

I think the best part is the pelican carrying the myna or pigeon to its destination. The addenda are fantastic, only adding to what's been established, and the whole thing is a consistent, madcap, perfect article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally really dislike wacky/zany SCPs but the tone's spot-on and the idea of a bird-universe is cool. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the caption.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, you just can't stop raven about how much you like this one, can you.

Seriously, though. I've always had a special appreciation for articles that just go hard for the weird. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recalled reading this as a draft once, liked it then, and like it now. Here's another for the absurdist school of SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Implying that there's a whole universe made entirely of birds… you got all the wat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Using SCP-2232 to make calls or send text messages must be done in the presence of a certified bird handler.

..and then you had me hooked. This is deep in stoves-limes-coffee-makers-and-pony-trams territory here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Android 5.7.4 "Delicious Sunflower Seeds".

upvoted here, nothing I saw afterwards would have prompted me to change my mind. This is a great SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2232-01 being used to make a phone call.

That picture though. That alone made me go "what" and read everything. Very nice hook.

Lesson learned: One should not overcharge birds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cute and weird from the fucking start, and it doesn't stop getting weirder and cuter. The exploding birdflesh part was definitely weird and was definitely an "eh" thing to me, but it doesn't really bring the article down. Some of the interview feels forced dialogue-wise, but again, it's not in a way worth changing, because it hammers the joke in really well. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image credits. Super thanks to Iruel for the idea! Ireul, to whom I owe one pizza.

This was sitting in my sandbox for quite a while, so I decided to give it a quick dusting-off and slap it onto the site. Let's hope it sticks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was interesting. Then I got to the transcript of the phone call.

+1. And take a bonus +1 for manfully (personfully?) resisting the temptation to make a joke about tweeting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Birds replacing things? Toucan play this game. Owl say though, I don't egret upvoting this article. Time and ptarmigan, your writing impresses, and that's nothing to grouse about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it as a draft, like it now.
